items.title: Type aliases and API clarity: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? A typedef creates a new strongly distinct type that prevents accidental mixing with its underlying type.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. typedef creates a type alias, not a new distinct type; enforce domain separation through APIs, structures, and validation rather than the alias alone.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. typedef creates a type alias, not a new distinct type; enforce domain separation through APIs, structures, and validation rather than the alias alone.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: typedef creates a type alias, not a new distinct type; enforce domain separation through APIs, structures, and validation rather than the alias alone.
A typedef creates a new strongly distinct type that prevents accidental mixing with its underlying type.

answer.explanation: typedef creates a type alias, not a new distinct type; enforce domain separation through APIs, structures, and validation rather than the alias alone.

items.title: Type aliases and API clarity: executable application
items.prompt: Use typedef to name a Point structure, read two points, and print their Manhattan distance while preserving the structure representation.
